title: hieracium murorum subsp. pseudoscutifolium
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. pseudoscutifolium, also known as Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family with a complex leaf structure and a yellow flower head. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Hawkweed include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and fighting off infections. Hawkweed is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, which can be applied topically to the affected area for optimal benefits.
